children.heading: Elderly, disabled, and near-elderly families
children.label: 1 Elderly, disabled, and near-elderly families
children.text: The terms “elderly family”, “disabled family”, and “near-elderly family” have the meanings given the terms under section 3(b)(3) of the United States Housing Act of 1937 [].42 U.S.C. 1437a(b)(3)

children.label: A
children.text: a public housing project (as such term is defined in section 3(b) of the United States Housing Act of 1937 []);42 U.S.C. 1437a(b)

children.label: B
children.text: housing for which project-based assistance is provided under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937 [];42 U.S.C. 1437f

children.label: C
children.text: housing that is assisted under ;section 1701q of title 12

children.label: D
children.text: housing that is assisted under , as such section existed before ;section 1701q of title 12November 28, 1990

children.label: E
children.text: housing financed by a loan or mortgage insured under section 1715(d)(3) of title 12 that bears interest at a rate determined under the proviso of section 1715(d)(5) of title 12;ll

children.label: F
children.text: housing insured, assisted, or held by the Secretary or a State or State agency under ;section 1715z–1 of title 12

children.label: G
children.text: housing constructed or substantially rehabilitated pursuant to assistance provided under section 8(b)(2) of the United States Housing Act of 1937 [], as in effect before , that is assisted under a contract for assistance under such section; and42 U.S.C. 1437f(b)(2)October 1, 1983

children.label: H
children.text: housing that is assisted under section 8013 of this title.1

children.heading: Housing assistance
children.label: 3 Housing assistance
children.text: The term “housing assistance” means, with respect to federally assisted housing, the grant, contribution, capital advance, loan, mortgage insurance, or other assistance provided for the housing under the provisions of law referred to in paragraph (2). The term also includes any related assistance provided for the housing by the Secretary, including any rental assistance for low-income occupants.

children.heading: Owner
children.label: 4 Owner
children.text: The term “owner” means, with respect to federally assisted housing, the entity or private person, including a cooperative or public housing agency, that has the legal right to lease or sublease dwelling units in such housing.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 5 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.
